messages.content:
Generate a thing of Uppertech's history timeline in bullets, with the year, a colon and what happened during it. Use this HTML's tech content:
Be prepared!
UDC 2001

With Uppertech's new uTrack a setout.

What we are selling
uCom, a nice computer.

uApper, which is more satisfying than I9K's Functioner.

uBinary, which can be downloaded for free!

uApi, a very low cost software.

- 2001: Uppertech releases the uTrack, a new setout
- 2001: UDC takes place and Uppertech showcases their products, including the uCom computer
- 2001: uApper is released, marketed as more satisfying than I9K's Functioner
- 2001: Uppertech releases uBinary as a free download
- 2001: uApi is released as a low-cost software option
See when it was founded (1987), early computers like the uII, uProgress and uTop Powered, and a few modern like the uDevice series, uDevice Fone and uDevice Tab are the ones used.

- 1987: Uppertech is founded
- Late 1980s: Early Uppertech computers like the uII, uProgress, and uTop Powered are released
- 2010: Uppertech releases the uDevice series
- 2012: The uDevice Fone is introduced
- 2015: Uppertech releases the uDevice Tab
Make separate years for the release, which is random between 1987 and 1995, and add the 2001 things

- 1987: Uppertech is founded
- Late 1980s: Early Uppertech computers like the uII, uProgress, and uTop Powered are released
- Early 1990s: Uppertech develops and releases new technology for their computers
- 2001: Uppertech releases the uTrack, a new setout
- 2001: UDC takes place and Uppertech showcases their products, including the uCom computer
- 2001: uApper is released, marketed as more satisfying than I9K's Functioner
- 2001: Uppertech releases uBinary as a free download
- 2001: uApi is released as a low-cost software option
- 2010: Uppertech releases the uDevice series
- 2012: The uDevice Fone is introduced
- 2015: Uppertech releases the uDevice Tab
Make more and more of years

- 1987: Uppertech is founded by a group of technology enthusiasts
- Late 1980s: Early Uppertech computers like the uII, uProgress, and uTop Powered are released, gaining popularity among tech-savvy individuals
- Early 1990s: Uppertech develops and releases new technology for their computers, including the uMouse and uKeyboard
- Mid 1990s: Uppertech expands their product line and starts offering computer accessories such as printers and scanners
- Late 1990s: Uppertech invests in research and development to create more advanced and innovative products
- 2001: Uppertech releases the uTrack, a new setout, which is well-received by consumers
- 2001: UDC takes place and Uppertech showcases their products, including the uCom computer, uApper software, uBinary for download, and uApi for purchase
- 2003: Uppertech launches their online store, allowing customers to easily purchase their products
- 2005: Uppertech partners with a leading tech company to produce the uPrint, a high-quality and affordable printer
- 2007: Uppertech introduces the uScanner, a versatile and user-friendly scanner
- 2009: Uppertech develops the uDrive, an external hard drive with large storage capacity
- 2010: Uppertech releases the uDevice series, which includes the uDevice Mini and uDevice Pro
- 2012: The uDevice Fone is introduced, and becomes a popular choice among smartphone users
- 2015: Uppertech releases the uDevice Tab, a top-of-the-line tablet with advanced features
Add years from 2023+ and various models like uTosh More, uTosh Colour, and uTosh original from earliest to latest like the Macintosh models.

- 1987: Uppertech is founded by a group of technology enthusiasts
- Late 1980s: Early Uppertech computers like the uII, uProgress, and uTop Powered are released, gaining popularity among tech-savvy individuals
- Early 1990s: Uppertech develops and releases new technology for their computers, including the uMouse and uKeyboard
- Mid 1990s: Uppertech expands their product line and starts offering computer accessories such as printers and scanners
- Late 1990s: Uppertech invests in research and development to create more advanced and innovative products
- 2001: Uppertech releases the uTrack, a new setout, which is well-received by consumers
- 2001: UDC takes place and Uppertech showcases their products, including the uCom computer, uApper software, uBinary for download, and uApi for purchase
- 2003: Uppertech launches their online store, allowing customers to easily purchase their products
- 2005: Uppertech partners with a leading tech company to produce the uPrint, a high-quality and affordable printer
- 2007: Uppertech introduces the uScanner, a versatile and user-friendly scanner
- 2009: Uppertech develops the uDrive, an external hard drive with large storage capacity
- 2010: Uppertech releases the uDevice series, which includes the uDevice Mini and uDevice Pro
- 2012: The uDevice Fone is introduced, and becomes a popular choice among smartphone users
- 2015: Uppertech releases the uDevice Tab, a top-of-the-line tablet with advanced features
- 2023: Uppertech introduces the uTosh More, a sleek and powerful desktop computer
- 2025: The uTosh Colour is released, offering customizability and a wide range of colors
- 2028: Uppertech releases the uTosh Original, a faithful reproduction of the original uTosh model from 1989
- 2032: The uTosh More II is released, boasting even more advanced features and hardware than its predecessor
- 2036: Uppertech introduces the uTosh Top, a compact and powerful option for those short on desk space
- 2040: The uTosh Edge is released, featuring a futuristic design and cutting-edge technology for the next generation of computing.

Add loads of more years and see projects of betas of the uOS systems.

- 1987: Uppertech is founded by a group of technology enthusiasts
- 1988: Uppertech releases the uII2, a more advanced version of their early computer model
- 1989: The uTosh is released, becoming Uppertech's most popular computer model to date
- Early 1990s: Uppertech develops and releases new technology for their computers, including the uMouse and uKeyboard
- Mid 1990s: Uppertech expands their product line and starts offering computer accessories such as printers and scanners
- Late 1990s: Uppertech invests in research and development to create more advanced and innovative products
- 2001: Uppertech releases the uTrack, a new setout, which is well-received by consumers
- 2001: UDC takes place and Uppertech showcases their products, including the uCom computer, uApper software, uBinary for download, and uApi for purchase
- 2003: Uppertech launches their online store, allowing customers to easily purchase their products
- 2005: Uppertech partners with a leading tech company to produce the uPrint, a high-quality and affordable printer
- 2007: Uppertech introduces the uScanner, a versatile and user-friendly scanner
- 2009: Uppertech develops the uDrive, an external hard drive with large storage capacity
- 2010: Uppertech releases the uDevice series, which includes the uDevice Mini and uDevice Pro
- 2012: The uDevice Fone is introduced, and becomes a popular choice among smartphone users
- 2015: Uppertech releases the uDevice Tab, a top-of-the-line tablet with advanced features
- 2017: Uppertech begins development on their own operating system, uOS, hoping to compete with industry giants like Windows and MacOS
- 2019: The first beta version of uOS is released to select testers and developers
- 2020: Uppertech opens up beta testing to the public, allowing anyone to try out uOS and provide feedback
- 2023: Uppertech introduces the uTosh More, a sleek and powerful desktop computer
- 2025: The uTosh Colour is released, offering customizability and a wide range of colors
- 2028: Uppertech releases the uTosh Original, a faithful reproduction of the original uTosh model from 1989
- 2030: The second version of uOS is released, incorporating feedback from beta testers and introducing new features
- 2032: The uTosh More II is released, boasting even more advanced features and hardware than its predecessor
- 2034: Uppertech introduces uTosh Lite, a budget-friendly desktop computer for casual users
- 2036: Uppertech introduces the uTosh Top, a compact and powerful option for those short on desk space
- 2038: The third version of uOS is released, with major improvements to performance and user interface
- 2040: The uTosh Edge is released, featuring a futuristic design and cutting-edge technology for the next generation of computing
- 2042: Uppertech releases a new beta version of uOS, promising even more advanced features and improved performance
- 2045: After years of development and testing, uOS 4.0 is released to the public, solidifying Uppertech's place as a major player in the software industry.
Add more years and more things like uFone (old, which has it's own system before a slim release of the uDevice) in 2005, and a few others
